title: HBR_VIOLATION typed receipt schema (CX-130 conformant)
goal: Define and ship the canonical HBR_VIOLATION typed receipt schema per spec §5.6.3 (AC-HBR-TYPED-RECEIPT). Receipts MUST carry hbr_id, wp_id, mt_id, role, evaluation_point in {build,handoff}, evidence_pointer, violation_class; freeform prose is never the wire contract and may accompany as `notes` only. Both Rust (for runtime emission) and Node (for build-time emission) emitters land together so wire format is identical.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust types in src/backend/handshake_core/src/hbr/violation.rs; matching Node module .GOV/roles_shared/scripts/hbr-violation-emit.mjs; JSON schema file .GOV/roles_shared/schemas/hbr-violation.schema.json; tests proving identical wire output cross-language.
unblock_conditions: MT-003 build-time emitter caller exists; MT-004 runtime emitter caller exists; MT-005 validator-scan caller exists — all three need a single canonical receipt schema.
implementation_notes: Per spec §5.6.3 and the CX-130 typed-routing principle (HBR-SWARM-004 citation), violation receipts must be parseable wire contracts. Schema: `{ receipt_kind: "HBR_VIOLATION", schema_version: 1, receipt_uuid: Uuid v7, hbr_id: "HBR-*", wp_id: string, mt_id: string|null, role: enum(ORCHESTRATOR|KERNEL_BUILDER|CODER|WP_VALIDATOR|INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR|VALIDATOR|CLASSIC_ORCHESTRATOR), evaluation_point: enum(build|handoff), evidence_pointer: string|null (URI per MT-005), violation_class: enum(MISSING_EVIDENCE|EVIDENCE_KIND_MISMATCH|EVIDENCE_PROOF_FAILED|APPLICABILITY_MISCONFIG|DOWNGRADE_ATTEMPT|MATRIX_SCHEMA_VIOLATION), emitted_at_utc: RFC3339, source_session: string|null, notes: string|null }`. Rust impl: serde derive on HbrViolation struct; `HbrViolation::emit(self, sink: &dyn ViolationSink)` writes a JSONL row to the configured sink (EventLedger row for runtime; RECEIPTS.jsonl for build-time). Node impl: emit() function with identical field shape; both write canonical-JSON (sorted keys, UTF-8 no BOM, terminating newline) so wire output is byte-identical. JSON schema file authoritative; both Rust serde test and Node ajv test validate sample fixture against it. Per HBR-INT-008 receipt_uuid is v7. The MT-003/MT-004/MT-005 callers convert their existing internal failure structs to HbrViolation before writing. Tests: round-trip Rust HbrViolation -> JSON -> Node parse -> Node HbrViolation == original; schema validates a violation with all violation_class variants.
risk_if_missed: Without typed-receipt schema, every emitter (build-time, runtime, validator-scan) invents its own field layout; downstream consumers cannot parse violations reliably; CX-130 typed-routing is violated.
validator_focus: Schema is the wire contract: Rust and Node both validate against the same .schema.json file; canonical-JSON output is byte-identical cross-language.

minimum_controls: JSON schema is the wire authority; both Rust and Node validate output against it in CI before emission.
Canonical-JSON (sorted keys, UTF-8 no BOM, terminating newline) is enforced by a serializer wrapper, not relied on at call sites.
Notes field is optional and must NEVER be the only carrier of routing info.
